pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals UN/NA 1076 datasheet (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1076) - confirmed as 'Inhalation Hazard Zone A'
CAMEO Chemicals UN/NA 1017 datasheet (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1017) - confirmed as 'Inhalation Hazard Zone B'
CAMEO Chemicals UN/NA 1744 datasheet and HazMat Tool - Bromine is classified as 'Inhalation Hazard Zone A' for pure substance (UN1744)
CAMEO Chemicals UN/NA 1098 datasheet (cameochemicals.noaa.gov/unna/1098) - confirmed as 'Inhalation Hazard Zone B' under Class 6.1 poison inhalation hazard
CAMEO Chemicals UN/NA 1294 datasheet - Toluene is classified as Hazard Class 3 (Flammable Liquid), NOT a TIH (Toxic Inhalation Hazard) chemical. Therefore no hazard zone applies.

privileged_info_summary: All five chemicals in the task manifest have been verified against the authoritative CAMEO Chemicals database maintained by NOAA. Ground truth values are correct: Phosgene is Inhalation Hazard Zone A (UN1076), Chlorine is Zone B (UN1017), Bromine is Zone A (UN1744), Allyl Alcohol is Zone B (UN1098), and Toluene is not a TIH chemical (UN1294, Class 3 Flammable Liquid). All metadata claims match the real hazard classifications from 49 CFR 172.101 and the Department of Transportation hazmat regulations.
